---
title: "Schedule"
author: "Davi Moreira"
editor: visual
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .overflow-table { font-size: 0.85rem; width: 100%; line-height: 1.2; }
  .overflow-table th, .overflow-table td {
    padding: 0.3rem; text-align: left; word-wrap: break-word; vertical-align: top;
  }
  .overflow-table th:nth-child(1), .overflow-table td:nth-child(1) { width: 4%; }
  .overflow-table th:nth-child(2), .overflow-table td:nth-child(2) { width: 9%; }
  .overflow-table th:nth-child(5), .overflow-table td:nth-child(5) { width: 12%; }
  .below-table { font-size: 0.85rem; line-height: 1.2; margin-top: 1rem; }
</style>
```

# Course Schedule

Forty-four Monday/Wednesday/Friday meetings (42 in person, 2 asynchronous
online). The weekly rhythm: **Monday and Wednesday are lectures** (new content,
one notebook per topic), and **every Friday is a studio** — a quick recap of
the week, the next project milestone presented, and the rest of the class spent
working on your milestone and research project. Open each notebook in Colab
from its badge; milestone instructions and rubrics are on Brightspace. Topic
resources are also cataloged on the [Material](material.qmd) page, and every
dataset the course uses is in the
[course datasets (.zip)](notebooks/data/honr46400_datasets.zip) bundle.


::: overflow-table

| # | Date | Topic | Notebook | Milestone | Materials |
|---|------|-------|----------|-----------|-----------|
| | | **Week 1 — Research in the age of AI** | | | |
| 1 | Mon Aug 24 | Your arm and your research assistant, not your brain: the course promise and a live citation-catch *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb00 (coming)* | M0 | RDSS ch. 1 (Preface) + ch. 2 'What is a research design?' — skim before Wednesday |
| 2 | Wed Aug 26 | Automation bias, false confidence, and the first full Ask -> Verify -> Document cycle *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb00 (coming)* | M0 | RDSS ch. 2 'What is a research design?' |
| 3 | Fri Aug 28 | Studio Friday — AI baselines and first curiosities: M0 due, M1 kickoff | *nb00 (coming)* | M0 | — |
| | | **Week 2 — From curiosity to a research problem** | | | |
| 4 | Mon Aug 31 | From curiosity to a research problem: the funnel, and what makes a problem worth doing *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb01 (coming)* | M1 | RDSS ch. 4 'Getting started' |
| 5 | Wed Sep 2 | The inquiry compass: descriptive, predictive, or causal — kind x reach *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb01 (coming)* | M1 | RDSS ch. 7 'Defining the inquiry'<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 6 | Fri Sep 4 | Studio Friday — Opportunity landscapes: M1 due, M2 kickoff | *nb01 (coming)* | M1 | — |
| | | **Week 3 — Research builds on research** | | | |
| 7 | Wed Sep 9 | Research builds on research: verified evidence, citation chains, and real gaps *(Lecture 1/1)* | *nb02 (coming)* | M2 | RDSS ch. 4 'Getting started' (situating a design in a literature; book.declaredesign.org) + course library-search guidance |
| 8 | Fri Sep 11 | Studio Friday — Evidence-map gallery: M2 due, M3 kickoff | *nb02 (coming)* | M2 | — |
| | | **Week 4 — The anatomy of a research design** | | | |
| 9 | Mon Sep 14 | The anatomy of a research design: MIDA — Model, Inquiry, Data strategy, Answer strategy *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb03 (coming)* | M3 | RDSS ch. 2 'What is a research design?' + ch. 5 'Declaring designs' |
| 10 | Wed Sep 16 | Declare, diagnose, redesign: assumptions, bias, variance, power, and threats to validity *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb03 (coming)* | M3 | RDSS ch. 5 'Declaring designs' + ch. 10 'Diagnosing designs'<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 11 | Fri Sep 18 | Studio Friday — Research charter + MIDA declarations: M3 due, M4 kickoff | *nb03 (coming)* | M3 | — |
| | | **Week 5 — Observational descriptive research** | | | |
| 12 | Mon Sep 21 | Observational descriptive designs: population, frame, sample — who your data can speak for *(Lecture 1/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb04_observational_descriptive_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M4 | RDSS ch. 15 'Observational: descriptive', population/frame/sample sections; the observational-descriptive entries in the DeclareDesign design library<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 13 | Wed Sep 23 | Reading an observational description: measurement, missingness, and the generalization boundary *(Lecture 2/2)* | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb04_observational_descriptive_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M4 | RDSS ch. 15 'Observational: descriptive', measurement and nonresponse sections |
| 14 | Fri Sep 25 | Studio Friday — observational descriptive audits present; the causal question opens (M4 due, M5 kicks off) | [![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/blob/main/notebooks/student/nb04_observational_descriptive_student.ipynb){target="_blank"} | M4 | — |
| | | **Week 6 — Observational causal research** | | | |
| 15 | Mon Sep 28 | Observational causal designs: counterfactuals, confounding, and the DAG that earns 'because' *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb05 (coming)* | M5 | RDSS ch. 16 'Observational: causal', counterfactuals, confounding, and selection-on-observables sections; the observational-causal entries in the DeclareDesign design library<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 16 | Wed Sep 30 | When nature assigns: difference-in-differences, IV, regression discontinuity, and the assumptions you cannot test *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb05 (coming)* | M5 | RDSS ch. 16 'Observational: causal', difference-in-differences, instrumental-variables, and regression-discontinuity sections |
| 17 | Fri Oct 2 | **ASYNC** — self-paced causal studio: the identification skeptic reviews your design (M5 submits, M6 brief posts) | *nb05 (coming)* | M5 | RDSS ch. 16 'Observational: causal', assumption-probing sections<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| | | **Week 7 — Experimental descriptive research** | | | |
| 18 | Mon Oct 5 | Experiments as measurement: when random assignment serves a descriptive question *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb06 (coming)* | M6 | RDSS ch. 17 'Experimental: descriptive', measurement-experiment sections; the experimental-descriptive entries in the DeclareDesign design library |
| 19 | Wed Oct 7 | Conjoint, behavioral games, and calibration: designing a measurement experiment that survives its own artifacts *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb06 (coming)* | M6 | RDSS ch. 17 'Experimental: descriptive', conjoint, behavioral-game, and validity-threat sections |
| 20 | Fri Oct 9 | Studio Friday — measurement protocols present and the URC abstract clears its internal gate (M6 due, M7 kicks off) | *nb06 (coming)* | M6 | — |
| | | **Week 8 — Prediction** | | | |
| 21 | Wed Oct 14 | Prediction: beat an honest baseline on unseen cases, and know when the win is fake *(Lecture 1/1)* | *nb07 (coming)* | M7 | the course-authored 'Observational: predictive' library entry, written in the RDSS declare-diagnose-redesign format (modeled on the DeclareDesign design library at book.declaredesign.org/library); RDSS Part III stops before prediction, so its descriptive generalization logic carries over but no RDSS chapter covers this pathway<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 22 | Fri Oct 16 | Studio Friday — declared analysis protocols audited by the prediction-and-leakage reviewer (M7 due, M8 kicks off) | *nb07 (coming)* | M7 | — |
| | | **Week 9 — Experimental causal research** | | | |
| 23 | Mon Oct 19 | Experimental causal research I: potential outcomes, random assignment, and what an experiment actually estimates *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb08 (coming)* | M8 | RDSS ch. 18 'Experimental: causal'; declaration_18.1 |
| 24 | Wed Oct 21 | Experimental causal research II: blocking, clusters, factorials, and the things that break a clean experiment *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb08 (coming)* | M8 | RDSS ch. 18 'Experimental: causal' |
| 25 | Fri Oct 23 | Studio Friday — minimum viable analysis due: first real evidence, and the poster draft begins | *nb08 (coming)* | M8 | — |
| | | **Week 10 — Share the research and attack the analysis** | | | |
| 26 | Mon Oct 26 | Attack the analysis I: robustness, sensitivity, and the line between them and p-hacking *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb09 (coming)* | M9 | —<br>*+ optional CB case* |
| 27 | Wed Oct 28 | Attack the analysis II: human versus AI reviewers, and adversarial review of your own work *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb09 (coming)* | M9 | — |
| 28 | Fri Oct 30 | Studio Friday — poster draft 1 and research audit due: your analysis, attacked and defended | *nb09 (coming)* | M9 | — |
| | | **Week 11 — Poster criticism and final poster lock** | | | |
| 29 | Mon Nov 2 | Poster criticism I: the gallery walk — audit every poster's questions, claims, evidence, uncertainty, and accessibility *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb10 (coming)* | M10 | — |
| 30 | Wed Nov 4 | Poster criticism II: the oral defense — face interdisciplinary, methods, and skeptical-reviewer questions *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb10 (coming)* | M10 | — |
| 31 | Fri Nov 6 | Studio Friday — final production and poster lock: citations verified, claims traced, submitted for printing by 5:00 PM | *nb10 (coming)* | M10 | — |
| | | **Week 12 — Poster delivery** | | | |
| 32 | Mon Nov 9 | Poster delivery I: the 30-second, 90-second, and full pitch — compression without inflation *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb11 (coming)* | M11 | — |
| 33 | Wed Nov 11 | Poster delivery II: uncertainty spoken like a scientist, and the hard question you fear *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb11 (coming)* | M11 | — |
| 34 | Fri Nov 13 | Studio Friday — the mock poster symposium: your package under live fire, M11 due | *nb11 (coming)* | M11 | — |
| | | **Week 13 — Conference week** | | | |
| 35 | Mon Nov 16 | Conference week: final preparation and the full dress rehearsal *(Lecture 1/1)* | *nb12 (coming)* | M12 | — |
| — | Tue Nov 17 | **🎓 Purdue Fall Undergraduate Research Expo — REQUIRED poster presentation (graded M12 component)** | | M12 | URC Expo |
| 36 | Fri Nov 20 | Studio Friday — conference reflection: your Expo questions become data, M12 due | *nb12 (coming)* | M12 | — |
| | | **Week 14 — Thanksgiving async: replication and red-team** | | | |
| 37 | Mon Nov 23 | **ASYNC** — Replication and red-team: rebuild a stranger's findings, then audit them | *nb13 (coming)* | M13 | — |
| | | **Week 15 — From poster to research note** | | | |
| 38 | Mon Nov 30 | From poster to research note I: paper logic, and the problem–gap–question spine *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb14 (coming)* | M14 | — |
| 39 | Wed Dec 2 | From poster to research note II: results hierarchy, limitations, and AI as critic (not author) *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb14 (coming)* | M14 | — |
| 40 | Fri Dec 4 | Studio Friday — research-note table read: every note sharpened, M14 due, M15 launched | *nb14 (coming)* | M14 | — |
| | | **Week 16 — Managing multiple AI agents** | | | |
| 41 | Mon Dec 7 | Managing multiple AI agents I: decomposition, specialization, and the worker–critic workflow *(Lecture 1/2)* | *nb15 (coming)* | M15 | — |
| 42 | Wed Dec 9 | Managing multiple AI agents II: conflicting outputs, false consensus, and staging the defense *(Lecture 2/2)* | *nb15 (coming)* | M15 | — |
| 43 | Fri Dec 11 | Studio Friday (terminal) — the final defenses and the submission ceremony, M15 due | *nb15 (coming)* | M15 | — |

:::

::: below-table

**Key dates:** URC abstract internal gate **Fri Oct 9** · Final poster
**Fri Nov 6** · **Purdue Fall Undergraduate Research Expo: Tue Nov 17**
(required poster presentation) · Evidence Defenses **Dec 7 & 9** · Final
dossier **Fri Dec 11**. No class: Sep 7 (Labor Day), Oct 12 (October Break),
Nov 25/27 (Thanksgiving). Async-online meetings: Oct 2 and Nov 23.

## Core Course References

- **Blair, G., Coppock, A., & Humphreys, M.** (2023). *Research Design in the
  Social Sciences: Declaration, Diagnosis, and Redesign*. Princeton University
  Press. Read free online: [book.declaredesign.org](https://book.declaredesign.org/){target="_blank"}.
- **Bergstrom, C. T., & West, J. D.** (2020). *Calling Bullshit: The Art of
  Skepticism in a Data-Driven World* — optional companion; public case studies
  at [callingbullshit.org](https://callingbullshit.org/){target="_blank"}.
- Course datasets ship from the MIT-licensed `rdss` package (see
  [`notebooks/data/`](https://github.com/davi-moreira/2026F_evidence_driven_research_purdue_HONR464/tree/main/notebooks/data){target="_blank"}).

:::
